summary: Moved the static BSS declaration for gm_80479C30 earlier in src/melee/gm/gm_1A36.static.h to restore BSS ordering and fix broken matches introduced by PR #1535. The change was minimal: gm_controller_map gm_80479C30[4] was relocated from the later static-data block near controller_union to an earlier position after struct gm_804D6840_t.
what_changed: Relocated the declaration `/* 479C30 */ static gm_controller_map gm_80479C30[4];` within `gm_1A36.static.h`.
Removed `gm_80479C30` from the later block of static declarations near `gm_803DD2C0`, `controller_union`, `gm_80479D20`, and `gm_80479D24`.
Inserted `gm_80479C30` immediately after `struct gm_804D6840_t` and before local static function prototypes such as `gm_801A3F48`, `gm_801A4014`, and `gm_801A4284`.
The PR body states this fixes broken matches from PR #1535.
smart_moves: Used a declaration-only relocation rather than changing code logic, keeping the fix tightly scoped to BSS layout/matching.
Preserved the address comment `/* 479C30 */`, making the intended BSS placement explicit and searchable.
Kept the array type and symbol name unchanged, reducing risk while correcting ordering.
decomp_lessons: For static headers that aggregate file-local data, declaration order can affect BSS layout and therefore matching.
When a prior cleanup or decomp PR breaks matches, inspect static/BSS declaration ordering before changing generated code or function bodies.
Address comments on static declarations are useful anchors for detecting and repairing misplaced BSS symbols.
A one-line relocation in a `.static.h` file can be the correct fix for match regressions when the underlying issue is object layout rather than codegen.
naming_conventions: Game-mode symbols in this area use the `gm_` prefix plus address-style suffixes, e.g. `gm_80479C30`, `gm_801A3F48`, `gm_803DD2C0`.
Static data declarations are annotated with address comments such as `/* 479C30 */`.
The affected type name `gm_controller_map` follows the subsystem-prefixed lowercase typedef style.
Nearby scene-related prototypes use typed scene names such as `MinorScene*` and `MajorScene*`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Correct BSS ordering by moving the static declaration to the position that matches the expected link order.
Prefer preserving symbol identity and type while only changing declaration order when fixing BSS layout mismatches.
Use address annotations in static headers to compare expected order against actual declaration order.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text states: `Fixes broken matches from https://github.com/doldecomp/melee/pull/1535`.
follow_up_queries: Inspect PR #1535 to identify what change disturbed gm_1A36 BSS ordering.
Search for other `.static.h` files where address comments indicate BSS declarations may be out of order.
Check whether `controller_union` and nearby `gm_80479D20`/`gm_80479D24` declarations still match expected BSS layout after this relocation.
